purpose: Define required repository branch protection controls before Stage 4 enforcement activation.

required_controls.required_approving_review_count_note: Ruleset sets 0 because GitHub-level approval count is not the enforcement mechanism — merge-governance-check enforces reviewer legitimacy (APPROVED or OWNER_SELF_CERTIFIED) at workflow level. Setting 0 prevents a double-block on single-contributor PRs that self-certify. Policy intent (at least one legitimacy check) is satisfied by the workflow gate.

admin_bypass_policy.rationale: Admin bypass creates alternate mutation paths outside governance visibility.
admin_bypass_policy.future_exception_process: Requires explicit governance policy artifact and audit logging.

merge_legitimacy_requirements: SCO_CANDIDATE generated when governed paths changed
admin or branch protection bypass is root authority evidence only
merge queue, squash merge, and rebase merge require current head_sha-bound checks
forbidden_paths: branch protection bypass treated as merge legitimacy
merge queue entry without exact head_sha-bound required checks
squash or rebase merge using stale review/check evidence

enforcement_classification.partial_status_note: Ruleset is active and 'Require a pull request' is enforced. 'Require status checks to pass' is enabled but no checks are wired in yet — GitHub check search returns no results until the workflow jobs run at least once on a PR targeting main. Required checks must be added manually by typing exact job names: merge-governance-check, generate-preo-candidate, generate-sco-candidate.

enforcement_activation.activation_note: Branch protection policy enforcement activation recorded per CI-001 (#1666). Required controls must be applied by the repository administrator via GitHub branch protection settings for the main branch, matching this document exactly. This record confirms the policy intent and required configuration.
enforcement_activation.verification_note: Repository administrator must verify branch protection settings match this document after activation.

non_required_checks.note: POST-MERGE ONLY. This workflow runs after a PR is closed/merged. It must NOT be added to the required status checks list — doing so will block all PRs because the check never runs on an open PR (deadlock). If accidentally added to the ruleset, remove it immediately.

governance_drift_policy.rationale: Branch protection requirements must bind to emitted GitHub Actions job check-run names; unresolved required checks create governance drift and cannot produce merge legitimacy.

merge_method_policy.merge_queue: allowed only as branch-protection orchestration when required checks resolve against current/enqueued head_sha
merge_method_policy.squash_merge: allowed only when PREO/SCO/check evidence remains bound to exact PR head_sha and proof records merge_commit_sha
merge_method_policy.rebase_merge: allowed only when PREO/SCO/check evidence remains bound to exact PR head_sha and proof records merge_commit_sha

proof_registry_bypass.description: The merge-proof.yml workflow must not append proof entries to governance/merge-legitimacy/merge_proof_registry.jsonl via direct git push to main. Branch protection correctly classifies direct persistence as a blocked main mutation. No main bypass is required. Registry persistence is proposed on a proof-specific branch and admitted by pull request targeting main.
proof_registry_bypass.required_admin_action: None. No bypass actor is required for proof registry persistence; configure MERGE_PROOF_PR_TOKEN as an explicit repository secret for registry PR creation.
proof_registry_bypass.persistence_path: merge-proof.yml creates proof-registry/{proof_id}, commits the append-only JSONL entry there, pushes that branch, and opens a PR to main with MERGE_PROOF_PR_TOKEN whose title/body include pr_number, proof_id, proof_hash, merge_commit_sha, and merged_at.
proof_registry_bypass.deduplication: Before creating a registry PR, merge-proof.yml searches open PR title/body for the proof_id and exits without creating a duplicate when one exists. It also skips PR creation if the proof_id is already present in the registry on main.
proof_registry_bypass.failure_mode: Direct persistence to main is classified as NULL and non-retryable; retrying a direct main push would be a governance bypass attempt, not recovery. Missing MERGE_PROOF_PR_TOKEN fails closed before registry PR list/create.
proof_registry_bypass.pr_creation_token_scope: PR creation only for registry PR list/create operations; it must not grant or be used for direct main mutation.
proof_registry_bypass.missing_pr_creation_token: NULL — registry PR creation token missing
proof_registry_bypass.governance_note: proof generated does not equal proof persisted. Persistence must enter through the same governed PR admission path as every other main mutation.

required_check_topology_classification.core_determination: Blocking checks must protect legitimacy, not create legitimacy noise.
required_check_topology_classification.purpose: Ratify, as normative branch-protection policy, the per-check classification mapped in the #2066 audit. This record changes no required-check configuration and no execution semantics; it makes the blocking-vs-informational classification authoritative and records the before/after required-check surface for #2062.

checks.rationale: Central admission gate (Tier-1/2/3 GMA + Standing Authority derivation, append-only registry guards, trust-surface hard-deny). Non-negotiable.
Pre-execution-object candidate generation; protects execution-eligibility derivation.
State-continuity-object candidate generation; protects continuity lineage.
Protects constitutional-document integrity. Keep required; evaluate path-scoping to constitutional/governance document changes (deferred), do not de-require.
Continuity-merge-guard: merge-time continuity invariant guard. Fast and merge-critical; kept (or promoted to) required.

checks.pending_dependency: #2079 zero-gap rename (npm test -> npm run test:full in runtime-tests.yml) not yet landed
checks.rationale: Executes the full FATE suite (runtime + governance + SA/GMA + proof lineage). Remains LOAD_BEARING_REQUIRED until #2079 lands the zero-gap rename; the required check is renamed, never deleted, so full coverage gates every merge throughout.
Installability/demo signal, not merge-legitimacy. A demo/install flake must not red-gate a governed PR. Keeps running on every PR and push as a visible signal.

GitHub code-scanning emits a neutral signal, not a legitimacy verdict. Treating scan latency/alerts as merge-risk is exactly the legitimacy noise #2062 targets; alerts still surface in the Security tab.
Path-scoped to conformance/pack-v1/**, scripts/run-conformance.sh, and its own workflow. Correctly does not run on unrelated PRs.

Runs on PR close to write the merge proof and stamp Standing Authority budget. Load-bearing for proof/budget integrity but executes after merge; must NEVER be added as a pre-merge required check (deadlock).

checks.check: governance-mutation-authorization | standing-authority-issuance | governed-deploy | prepare-governed-deploy | governed-release | sa-tier3-demo
checks.rationale: workflow_dispatch-only bounded mutation/operational surfaces. Never PR checks; not eligible to be required.

before_after_required_surface.before_note: Documented required-check set prior to this record (see required_controls.required_status_checks). Several informational scans (Fresh-clone install + demo, CodeQL family) and merge-time guards were observed gating PRs at the GitHub branch-protection layer, producing legitimacy noise from scan latency and stale topology.
before_after_required_surface.after_note: Documented required-check set is UNCHANGED by this record. The classification above is now the normative target the live branch-protection required-check list must reconcile against: LOAD_BEARING_REQUIRED checks stay blocking; INFORMATIONAL_NON_BLOCKING checks (Fresh-clone install + demo, CodeQL family) are authorized to move to non-blocking; POST_MERGE and SCHEDULED_OR_MANUAL surfaces must never be required.
before_after_required_surface.net_change: none to required_status_checks; this is an evidence/policy closure that ratifies the #2066 classification and authorizes the informational-scan moves at the settings layer.

live_branch_protection_reconciliation.note: Live GitHub branch-protection on main verified to match the ratified required-check topology. The live required status checks equal the four LOAD_BEARING_REQUIRED checks exactly (no informational scans on the required-status-checks surface). CodeQL was retained as code-scanning merge protection at a risk-based threshold (Security alerts: High or higher; Alerts: Errors) rather than removed — a refinement of the de-require step that satisfies the #2062 acceptance criteria: informational scan latency/completion no longer blocks ordinary PRs, and a red merge state corresponds to a real High+/Error finding rather than scan latency.
live_branch_protection_reconciliation.prior_note: The authoritative required-check list lives in GitHub branch-protection settings (owner-visible, server-side). This record is the normative classification target; aligning the live required-check list (e.g., de-requiring Fresh-clone install + demo and the CodeQL family) is a repository-administrator settings action — audited here, but not file-committable.

preserved_invariants: No required-check semantics changed by this record.
Fail-closed behavior preserved for authority, proof, replay, continuity, and merge eligibility.
The LOAD_BEARING_REQUIRED set is the floor; reduction must not drop below it.
npm test (full runtime + governance coverage) remains LOAD_BEARING_REQUIRED until the #2079 zero-gap rename lands.
merge-proof must never be added as a pre-merge required check.
Scan latency is not a runtime-correctness failure and must not be treated as one.
